items.title: Nepal's ex-PM arrested over fatal protest crackdown
items.summary: More than 70 people were killed during an uprising last year which ousted Oli's government.

items.title: 'The gravest crime against humanity': What does the UN vote on slavery mean?
items.summary: African and Caribbean nations want countries which benefited from slavery to pay compensation but it will not be straightforward.

items.title: Tiger Woods charged with driving under influence after crash
items.summary: Tiger Woods, the 15-time golf major champion, is released on bail following his arrest on charges of driving under the influence following a car crash in Florida.

items.title: US House to vote on DHS funding as travel chaos deepens
items.summary: The planned vote is unlikely to end the funding impasse as long security lines at US airports continue.

items.title: From jammed broadcasts to a blocked website: BBC Russian's 80 years of defiance
items.summary: For half a century, BBC Russian had to fend off jammers. Now its website is blocked, but millions still read it.

items.title: Iran-backed hackers breach FBI director Kash Patel's personal emails
items.summary: A hacker group shares Patel's purported resume and photos - information, the FBI says, is "historical in nature".

items.title: Rubio says US expects to finish Iran war 'in next couple of weeks'
items.summary: Marco Rubio said US allies were open to helping escort ships through the Strait of Hormuz, which Iran has closed and threatened to charge fees for.

items.title: Two Cuba-bound aid ships missing after leaving Mexico
items.summary: Mexico's navy launches a rescue mission for ships delivering aid to Cuba, strained by food and fuel shortages amid a US embargo.

items.title: Spanish woman who died through euthanasia failed by state, say critics
items.summary: Noelia Castillo died on Thursday evening in a Barcelona hospital, after a protracted legal battle with her own father.

items.title: Austria becomes latest to propose social media ban for children
items.summary: The proposed under-14 ban follows similar steps in other European countries, including France and Spain.

items.title: 'Our home is gone': BBC speaks to displaced families in Lebanon
items.summary: The BBC's Hugo Bachega speaks to families in Beirut as more than a million people have been displaced in Lebanon.

items.title: Three dead after helicopter crash in Hawaii
items.summary: The helicopter, which was carrying one pilot and four passengers,crash-landed about 100 yards off a beach, officials say.

items.title: Man arrested over plot to firebomb pro-Palestinian activist's NY home
items.summary: Agents found eight Molotov cocktails in the man's Hoboken home, according to court documents.

items.title: Belarus leader gifts rifle to North Korea's Kim as they sign friendship treaty
items.summary: Two key supporters of Russia's war in Ukraine close ranks as Alexander Lukashenko visits Pyongyang.

items.title: Savannah Guthrie to return to Today show as search for mother continues
items.summary: The US television presenter will return to hosting the NBC news programme in April.

items.title: Palestinian man killed as death toll from West Bank settler violence climbs
items.summary: Mohammad al-Malhi is the seventh Palestinian killed by a settler in a surge of violence since the start of the war with Iran.

items.title: UN human rights chief calls on US to conclude probe into Iran school strike
items.summary: The strike - which killed at least 168 people, mostly children - "evoked a visceral horror", Volker Türk said.

items.title: Whale swims for freedom after big German rescue effort on Baltic coast
items.summary: Rescue teams now believe the whale has reached deeper water in Lübeck Bay and hope it will head for the wider sea.

items.title: Nepal swears in ex-rapper as new prime minister
items.summary: Balendra Shah won the election by a landslide promising change to Nepalis who are angry at corruption.

items.title: I escaped North Korea with my mum. Now I'm terrified she might be sent back
items.summary: Geumseong survived a dangerous journey to South Korea, but his mother is stuck in a Chinese prison and may be forcibly repatriated.

items.title: 'Memory of a generation': China mourns the sudden death of a controversial education influencer
items.summary: The divisive 41-year-old was among the country's most prominent influencers.

items.title: Is Trump's pause on attacking Iranian energy for diplomacy or an escalation?
items.summary: The US president's commitment to deadlines is fluid but he uses them for a purpose, writes the BBC's James Landale.

items.title: 'My daughter is under the rubble': Inside Tehran as civilian toll of strikes rises
items.summary: One month since the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran, Tehran residents tell the BBC their lives have been devastated.

items.title: Three charts that are warning signs flashing for Trump on Iran war
items.summary: As gas prices rise, Trump's approval rating is heading into politically dangerous territory.

items.title: How Russia's threat has seen Germany become Europe's most important army
items.summary: Germany once terrorised Europe, but now Europe is welcoming a resurgent German military.

items.title: Women secretly filmed, then ridiculed and abused online
items.summary: Women in Kenya and Ghana tell the BBC about being approached by a Russian man who later posts videos of them without their consent.

items.title: 'Our home is gone': BBC speaks to displaced families in Lebanon
items.summary: The BBC's Hugo Bachega speaks to families in Beirut as more than a million people have been displaced in Lebanon.

items.title: Why is it so hard to pass through the Strait of Hormuz?
items.summary: Paul Adams explains why it is so dangerous to navigate the strait, one of the world's busiest oil shipping channels.

items.title: BBC journalist caught in travel chaos at Houston Airport
items.summary: The airport is experiencing some of the longest delays in the US as TSA agents work without pay in a partial shutdown.

items.title: Guthrie on missing mother: 'We cannot be at peace without knowing'
items.summary: In her first interview since the disappearance, Savannah Guthrie recounts the moment she learned her mother was missing and wrestles with the idea that her fame may have made her mother a target.

items.title: What happened in the seconds before Air Canada plane crashed at LaGuardia
items.summary: BBC Verify breaks down the moments before the deadly collision with a fire truck at the New York airport.
